framework.reasoning: This paper describes a discrete-event, agent-based limit-order-book simulation, not a gradient-trained neural network — there is no 'training' in the deep-learning sense. We nonetheless select PyTorch as the numerical backend (rather than pure NumPy) purely for its vectorized tensor ops, which let the LOB depth arrays and per-stock batches of agent state be updated on CPU or GPU with the same code path, and for its RNG/seeding utilities that are reused across the ArXivist repo template. No autograd, layers, or optimizers are used. Plain YAML (no Hydra/OmegaConf) is sufficient since the config is a flat set of simulation parameters.

module_hierarchy.description: Wires agents + LOB together into one full-stock simulation loop (the 'model' in the ABM sense).

public_methods.returns: trade tape, price series, per-day volume/range

public_methods.signature: (self, t: int, rng: np.random.Generator) -> None

module_hierarchy.description: 'Trainer' in the ArXivist template maps here to the batch simulation runner: iterates over N stocks (optionally parallel), running each StockMarketSimulation and collecting results. No gradient descent occurs.

public_methods.name: run_all_stocks
public_methods.signature: (self, stock_configs: list[StockConfig], scenario: str, n_jobs: int = -1) -> list[SimulationResult]

public_methods.name: run_counterfactual_suite
public_methods.signature: (self, stock_configs: list[StockConfig], scenarios: list[str]) -> dict[str, list[SimulationResult]]

module_hierarchy.description: Not applicable to a gradient-trained model. Repurposed to hold the impact-curve fitting objective (Eq. 3, relative least squares) since that is the paper's only 'loss'.

public_methods.name: fit
public_methods.signature: (self, x: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, float]

module_hierarchy.description: Impact-curve fitting, Hill-estimator tail exponents (beta, alpha), LOB depth-profile exponent (gamma), and the three theory predictors.

public_methods.name: log_bin_and_fit
public_methods.signature: (self, q_norm: np.ndarray, i_norm: np.ndarray, n_bins: int = 20, min_pts: int = 30) -> dict

public_methods.name: hill_estimator
public_methods.signature: (self, values: np.ndarray, tail_fraction: float = 0.1) -> float

public_methods.name: ggps_delta
public_methods.signature: (self, beta: float) -> float

public_methods.name: fglw_delta
public_methods.signature: (self, alpha: float) -> float

public_methods.name: lob_walking_delta
public_methods.signature: (self, gamma: float) -> float

module_hierarchy.description: YAML config loading, validation, and global seeding (Python/NumPy/PyTorch) for reproducibility.

public_methods.name: load
public_methods.signature: (cls, path: str) -> 'Config'

public_methods.name: set_global_seed
public_methods.signature: (seed: int, deterministic: bool = False) -> None

tensor_flows.name: Single-stock simulation loop

input.name: stock_config
input.shape: dict of scalars (P0, N_retail, N_HFT, xi, seed)

steps: Initialize LimitOrderBook at mid-price P0 with tick_size = 1bp * P0
Instantiate 20 InstitutionalAgent, N_HFT HFTMarketMakerAgent, N_retail RetailAgent, 3 NewsAgent
for t in range(warmup_steps + n_steps): pick one agent uniformly at random; agent.act(lob, t, rng)
Record every executed trade to trade_tape (post-warmup only)
Aggregate trade_tape into daily OHLCV (volume V_D, range sigma_D) for normalization

tensor_flows.name: Impact-curve estimation (per stock)
steps: ImpactCurveFitter.log_bin_and_fit(Q_norm, I_norm, n_bins=20, min_pts=30) -> (c, delta) via Eq. 3
TailExponentEstimator.hill_estimator on metaorder sizes -> beta; on child-order counts -> alpha
Fit LOB depth profile V(q) ~ q^(1+gamma) -> gamma
TheoryPredictors.{ggps,fglw,lob_walking}_delta(...) -> predicted delta for comparison against measured delta

tensor_flows.name: Counterfactual ablation suite
steps: For each scenario in [Baseline, No splitting, No HFT, Price limits, Low liquidity, Momentum, Uniform split, Front-loaded]:
Apply the scenario's single-component override to the base StockMarketSimulation config
Fit delta per stock as above; aggregate mean +/- SE across the 20 stocks
Assemble Table 1 (delta, c, N_meta, delta_GGPS, delta_FGLW, delta_LOB, gamma per scenario)

model.hft_adaptive_spread_rule: volatility_scaled  # ASSUMED: exact rule not specified in paper (SIR confidence 0.4)
model.news_trigger_rate: 0.001  # ASSUMED: low-frequency Poisson trigger, not specified (SIR confidence 0.35)
model.splitting_rule_default: dirichlet  # explicit name; exact distribution shape ASSUMED (SIR confidence 0.55)

training.convergence_check: compare delta on first vs second half of trajectory, tolerance 0.01  # explicit

evaluation.scenarios: [baseline, no_splitting, no_hft, price_limits, low_liquidity, momentum, uniform_split, front_loaded]

hardware.device: cpu  # default; GPU optional via torch, not required
hardware.num_workers: -1  # use all cores for embarrassingly-parallel per-stock runs
hardware.precision: float64  # simulation uses double precision for price/volume accumulation

runtime.reason: core array math, RNG (Generator API) for the ABM
optional vectorized/GPU array backend for batched stock simulation and global seeding utility, per ArXivist repo template
daily OHLCV aggregation, per-stock results tables
curve_fit for the relative-least-squares delta fit (Eq. 3), Hill estimator support
impact-curve and ablation bar-chart figures (Figures 3-7 style plots)
embarrassingly-parallel per-stock simulation runs (paper reports 40-core wall-clock time)

entrypoints.purpose: Run the full batch simulation (baseline or a named counterfactual scenario) across N stocks and write raw trade tapes + fitted delta/c per stock.

cli_args.help: Path to a partially-completed results checkpoint to resume from
One of the 8 scenarios in config_schema.evaluation.scenarios
Reduce n_stocks to 4 and n_steps to 5000 for a fast local smoke test
Build all components and validate config without running the simulation

entrypoints.purpose: Load saved per-stock results (from train.py), fit impact curves, compute theory predictions, and reproduce Table 1 / Figures 3-7.

cli_args.help: Where to write summary tables and figures

entrypoints.purpose: Run a single-stock simulation (one seed, one scenario) end-to-end and print its fitted delta/c — the fastest way to sanity-check a config change.
Paper-specific script: runs all 8 scenarios across the 20 counterfactual stocks and reproduces Table 1 and Figure 7 directly.

cli_args.help: Output directory for Table 1 and Figure 7

risk_assessment.description: HFT adaptive-spread rule and news-agent trigger process are not quantitatively specified in the paper (SIR confidence 0.4 / 0.35). An incorrect guess here directly affects the fitted gamma and the no-HFT ablation delta (0.386), which is one of the paper's two headline numbers.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement both as config-swappable strategies behind a small strategy interface (e.g. `spread_rule: 'volatility_scaled' | 'fixed_jitter'`) so a user can try alternatives without code changes; flagged clearly in README's Reproducibility Notes.

risk_assessment.description: Exact parameter values for the 'Price limits', 'Momentum', and 'Low liquidity' ablation scenarios are named but not numerically specified.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose each as explicit config fields with literature-standard defaults (e.g. 10% daily price band, 30-tick momentum lookback, HFT count halved for low liquidity) and comment them as ASSUMED in config.yaml.

risk_assessment.description: 'Dirichlet', 'Uniform', and 'Front-loaded' child-order splitting rules are named but not mathematically defined beyond their descriptive names.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement all three as swappable strategies in InstitutionalAgent.split_into_children with the most standard interpretation of each name, documented inline with the ambiguity noted.

risk_assessment.description: This is a stochastic simulation with no ground-truth loss to minimize; 'convergence' means the fitted delta stabilizes across trajectory halves, which is a much weaker guarantee than typical ML train/val convergence and can hide slow-moving bias.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reproduce the paper's own convergence check (compare delta on first vs. second half of each 1e6-step trajectory) as an automated assertion in evaluate.py, not just a one-off manual check.

risk_assessment.description: The exact price-time-priority matching engine internals (cancellation policy beyond HFT replenishment, tie-breaking) are common practice but not stated, which could shift the fine details of the depth profile gamma.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use a standard FIFO price-time-priority book (documented in lob.py docstring) and report gamma as an emergent, not hard-coded, quantity so any matching-engine sensitivity is visible in results rather than silently baked in.
